assumptions.priceNote: Reference notional cost uses seed price only and is not treated as executable market liquidity or final fair value.

scenarios.title: Single-bloc quorum capture under low turnout
scenarios.condition: A single aligned voting bloc reaches quorum while opposition turnout is negligible.
scenarios.repeatability: repeatable if quorum-sized bloc remains coordinated and opposition turnout stays weak
scenarios.maximalDamage: pass arbitrary queued proposals within timelock and governance scope
scenarios.mitigationStatus: blocked only by turnout, distribution discipline, signer review, and timelock monitoring

scenarios.title: TGE-era single-source quorum concentration
scenarios.condition: A single TGE source controls the full circulating quorum-sized tranche.
scenarios.repeatability: bounded to the launch circulating set but immediately material at TGE if concentration is not constrained
scenarios.maximalDamage: same as GOV-CAP-01, amplified by low early turnout
scenarios.mitigationStatus: requires distribution controls, signer review, and parameter acceptance

scenarios.title: Proposal spam workload
scenarios.condition: Proposal threshold remains zero so proposal creation is not token-gated.
scenarios.repeatability: repeatable with no token capital gate
scenarios.maximalDamage: governance reviewer fatigue, operational queue noise, and monitoring load
scenarios.mitigationStatus: unmitigated at contract threshold level; must be justified or changed

scenarios.title: Post-snapshot vote injection
scenarios.condition: Attacker tries to mint or delegate after snapshot to rescue an existing proposal.
scenarios.repeatability: not currently viable for an existing proposal under OZ Votes snapshot semantics
scenarios.mitigationStatus: mechanically mitigated by snapshot semantics and local executable tests
